entities.name: PROTEOME-WIDE FUNCTIONAL CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of classifying proteins across the entire proteome using bioinformatics to reveal unknown functions.

entities.name: BIOINFORMATICS APPROACHES
entities.description: Computational tools and techniques used to analyze and interpret biological data.

entities.name: PROTEIN FUNCTIONS
entities.description: The specific biological roles and activities performed by proteins within an organism.

entities.name: COMPUTATIONAL APPROACHES
entities.description: Algorithmic and mathematical methods used for biological data analysis and prediction.

entities.name: PROTEIN FUNCTION PREDICTION
entities.description: The use of computational models to infer the function of unknown proteins by integrating data sources.

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of genes (genome) of an organism.

entities.name: PROTEOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of cellular proteins, including their structures and functions.

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The process by which information from a gene is used in the synthesis of a functional gene product.

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: The basic physical and functional units of heredity.

entities.name: PROTEIN PRODUCTS
entities.description: The proteins synthesized as a result of gene expression.

entities.name: REGULATORY NETWORKS
entities.description: Complex systems of molecular interactions that control fundamental biological processes.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES
entities.description: A series of events that occur within a living organism to maintain life.

entities.name: CELLULAR PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins found within the cells of an organism, used to study genetic information.

entities.name: GENE FUNCTION
entities.description: The specific biological role played by a gene.

entities.name: GENETIC INFORMATION
entities.description: The hereditary information encoded in DNA.

entities.name: GENE-EXPRESSION PATTERNS
entities.description: The specific set of genes being expressed in a cell or tissue, used to identify signalling pathways or disease states.

entities.name: SIGNALLING PATHWAYS
entities.description: The series of chemical reactions in which a cell converts an external signal into a specific response.

entities.name: DISEASE STATES
entities.description: The physiological or pathological condition of an organism affected by a disease.
